subject: NSEC
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.q: Which of the following accounts best for the fact that $\mathrm{F}^{-}$is smaller than $\mathrm{O}^{2-}$ ?
options: F is more electronegative than O

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.q:
Which of the following is correct about the isoelectronic species, $\mathrm{Li}^{+}$and $\mathrm{H}^{-}$?
I. $\mathrm{H}^{-}$is larger in size than $\mathrm{Li}^{+}$
II. $\mathrm{Li}^{+}$is a better reducing agent than $\mathrm{H}^{-}$
III. It requires more energy to remove an electron from $\mathrm{H}^{-}$than from $\mathrm{Li}^{+}$
IV. The chemical properties of the two ions are the same

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.q: The energy required to remove an electron from a gaseous species ' X ' to form ' X '. is known as first ionization energy (IE) of X . The energy required to remove an electron from a gaseous species ' $\mathrm{X}^{+}$' to form ' $\mathrm{X}^{++}$' is called the second IE of X . Similarly, the energy required to remove an electron from a gaseous species $\mathrm{X}^{-}$to form X is called the IE of $\mathrm{X}^{-}$. Identify the correct statement/s from the following
options: The second IE of the He atom is four times that of the (first) IE of the H atom.
The first IEs of F, Ne and Na atoms follow the order IE $(\mathrm{Na}) < \mathrm{IE}(\mathrm{Ne}) < \mathrm{IE}(\mathrm{F})$
The second IE of the $\mathrm{H}^{-}$ion is much less than the (first) IE of the H atom.
The IEs of $\mathrm{Li}, \mathrm{Na}$ and K atoms follow the order $\mathrm{IE}(\mathrm{K}) < \mathrm{IE}(\mathrm{Na}) < \mathrm{IE}(\mathrm{Li})$

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.q: $\mathrm{N}^{3-}, \mathrm{F}^{-}, \mathrm{Na}^{+}$and $\mathrm{Mg}^{2+}$ have the same number of electrons. Which of them will have the smallest and the largest ionic radii respectively?

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.q:
Among the following, the correct statement/s about 'p' block elements is/are
I. The valence shell electronic configuration of all of them is $\mathrm{ns}^2 \mathrm{np}^{1-6}$
II. Only in $p$ block, metals, nonmetals and metalloids are present
III. Halogens have the lowest negative electron gain enthalpy in the respective periods
IV. Noble gases have no tendency to accept an electron and hence they have large negative values of electron gain enthalpy

questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.q: If an element after oganesson ( Og , atomic number 118 and electronic configuration [ Rn ] $5 \mathrm{f}^{14} 6 \mathrm{~d}^{10} 7 \mathrm{~s}^2 7 \mathrm{p}^6$ ) was discovered, in which of the following orbital, will the $119^{\text {th }}$ electron be accommodated?

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
questions.q:
Identify the correct matching of the following oxides in column $\mathbf{M}$ with their property in column $\mathbf{N}$ :
$\mathbf{M}$ $\mathbf{N}$
(i) Aluminium trioxide (p) Acidic oxide
(ii) Calcium oxide (q) Basic oxide
(iii) Arsenic pentoxide (r) Amphoteric oxide
